name: Marianet, Eslida, and Puntal de l'Aljub Loop
name_original: Parque Natural de la Serra d'Espadà — Vuelta por los puertos de Marianet, Eslida y Puntal de l'Aljub
description: The Marianet, Eslida, and Puntal de l'Aljub Loop is a challenging 32.9-mile road cycling route in Sierra de Espadán with significant climbs
region_intro: Road cycling routes around La Vall D'Uixó are situated in the province of Castellón, between the Mediterranean Sea and the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. This region offers diverse terrain, ranging from demanding mountainous areas with significant elevation gains to flatter routes leading towards the coast. The landscape is characterized by the Sierra de Espadán's cork tree forests and challenging passes, providing varied experiences for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around La Vall D'Uixó The most popular road cycling route is Marianet, Eslida, and Puntal de l'Aljub Loop, a 32.9 miles (52.9 km) trail that takes 2 hours 58 minutes to complete. This route traverses the Sierra de Espadán, featuring notable ascents. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Marianet Pass – Eslida Pass loop from la Vall d'Uixó, a difficult 42.9 miles (69.1 km) path. This route includes challenging climbs like the Eslida Pass, known for its views and average slopes. Local road cyclists also love the Via Augusta – Sagunto loop from la Vall d'Uixó, a 32.4 miles (52.1 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes. Road cycling around La Vall D'Uixó is defined by mountainous passes in the Sierra de Espadán, coastal routes, and diverse terrain. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The routes in La Vall D'Uixó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 1,600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore La Vall D'Uixó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around La Vall d'Uixó?
A: La Vall d'Uixó offers a diverse range of terrain for road cyclists. You'll find demanding mountainous areas with significant elevation gains, particularly within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, featuring challenging passes like the Puerto del Marianet and Eslida. There are also flatter routes that lead towards the Levantine coast and picturesque fishing villages, providing varied experiences from long climbs to more relaxed rides.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: Yes, experienced cyclists will find several challenging routes. The Sierra de Espadán Natural Park is a highlight, offering demanding ascents. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e881777553/marianet-eslida-and-puntal-de-l-aljub-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marianet, Eslida, and Puntal de l'Aljub Loop&lt;/a&gt; covers 52.9 km with over 1000 meters of elevation gain. Another difficult option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32610346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marianet Pass – Eslida Pass loop from la Vall d'Uixó&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 69.1 km with over 1400 meters of climbing, including the Eslida Pass known for its average slopes of 7% and spectacular views.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: Yes, La Vall d'Uixó offers routes suitable for various ability levels, including easy and moderate options. While many routes feature climbs, there are 9 easy routes available. For a moderate experience with less elevation, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24427460" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Via Augusta – Sagunto loop from la Vall d'Uixó&lt;/a&gt;, a 52.1 km trail with around 220 meters of elevation gain, often completed in about 2 hours 10 minutes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: While cycling, you can experience the cork tree forests and pure mountain air of the Sierra de Espadán. The region also features historical sites such as the Roman aqueducts of Sant Josep and l'Alcúdia. For natural attractions, you might pass by highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4431242" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ajuez Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4104060" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nules Pond&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also known for its mountain passes, including the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2472919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eslida Pass&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: La Vall d'Uixó benefits from mild weather conditions year-round, making it suitable for cycling throughout different seasons. This allows cyclists to enjoy the routes without extreme temperatures, whether you prefer the warmth of spring and autumn or the cooler days of winter.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: While many routes in the Sierra de Espadán are challenging, the region does offer easier options that could be suitable for families depending on their cycling experience. There are 9 routes classified as 'easy' in the area, which typically feature less elevation and shorter distances. It's always recommended to check the specific route details for distance and elevation to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in La Vall d'Uixó are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e881777553/marianet-eslida-and-puntal-de-l-aljub-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Marianet, Eslida, and Puntal de l'Aljub Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39563927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Torres Torres loop from Grupo Jesús Obrero&lt;/a&gt;, which is 54.7 km long.

Q: Is there convenient parking available for cyclists in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: La Vall d'Uixó is a town with various starting points for cycling routes. While specific dedicated cyclist parking isn't detailed, you can generally find public parking areas within the town or near popular trailheads. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: There are over 20 road cycling routes available around La Vall d'Uixó, offering a wide variety of experiences. These include 9 easy routes, 11 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes, catering to different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: The road cycling routes in La Vall d'Uixó are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from more than 20 reviews. Over 1,600 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging mountain passes, scenic views, and diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or food along the road cycling routes?
A: Many road cycling routes in La Vall d'Uixó pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops to refuel. The region's diverse terrain means you'll often encounter opportunities for a break, especially in the more populated areas or smaller villages nestled within the mountains.

Q: Where can cyclists find accommodation in La Vall d'Uixó?
A: La Vall d'Uixó, being a town, offers various accommodation options including hotels, guesthouses, and apartments suitable for cyclists. Its location also provides access to rural accommodations in the surrounding areas, especially for those looking to stay closer to the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park.
tips:
One of the ports of access to the Serra d'Espada that allows linking with the port of Eslida.
It is an ideal section for warming up, it does not have demanding slopes, the pavement is in good condition and there is usually little traffic. Clarify that in summer the sun will hit a lot because there is no shade on the route.

For those of you who don't know this climb, keep in mind that in the last 2 km the slope ranges between 10 and 17%, with peaks of 20%, with almost no breaks other than a couple of very short sections at 5 - 7%. . In summer, it is better to get up early, because the climb in full sun can end badly.

A classic in the Serra d'Espada, not only for its challenging climb but also for its spectacular views and landscapes throughout the journey. With average slopes of 7% and pavement in perfect condition, it is a mountain pass that you cannot miss!

faqs.question: How challenging is the Marianet, Eslida, and Puntal de l'Aljub Loop for road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This loop is considered difficult, designed for experienced road cyclists. It features significant climbs and descents, including over 1,000 meters of elevation gain over its 52.9 km distance. Be prepared for a demanding ride.

faqs.question: What are the main highlights and viewpoints along this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route offers magnificent natural scenery throughout the Sierra de Espadán. Key highlights include the challenging Marianet Pass and the popular Eslida Pass. From the Puntal de l'Aljub Summit, you can enjoy panoramic views that stretch all the way to the coast.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Marianet, Eslida, and Puntal de l'Aljub Loop?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Eslida is a popular starting point for cyclists in the region. You'll likely find suitable parking in or around the village of Eslida, which is well-known among the cycling community.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Espadán region generally offers pleasant cycling conditions outside of the peak summer heat. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any sections of the route that are particularly demanding or require special attention?
faqs.answer: Yes, the climb to Puntal de l'Aljub Summit is particularly notable. There's a three-kilometer stretch where the road narrows and the surface can become more challenging, requiring careful gearing. Some descriptions suggest that the very top might even require dismounting and pushing your bike for short sections, as it's not always perfectly smooth asphalt.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, and while dogs are generally allowed in the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park, bringing a dog on a challenging road bike loop like this is not typical or recommended for their safety or yours. If you're looking for dog-friendly trails, consider hiking routes in the park instead.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay an entrance fee to cycle in the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the Parque Natural de la Sierra de Espadán. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas, and stay on marked routes.

faqs.question: Does this cycling route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the [CIMA CS10] Puntal de l'Aljub * Xóvar and [CIMA CS10] Puntal de l'Aljub * Eslida, which are recognized cycling climbs. It also briefly touches parts of El Camí IT5TR8 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana and the Via Augusta de la Comunitat Valenciana.

faqs.question: Is this Marianet, Eslida, and Puntal de l'Aljub Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this route is explicitly rated as difficult and is not recommended for beginners. Its significant elevation gain and challenging terrain, especially on the climb to Puntal de l'Aljub, require a good level of fitness and cycling experience.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or places to refuel along the way?
faqs.answer: The Port of Eslida is a popular destination for cyclists, and the village of Eslida itself is likely to have options for cafes or places to refuel. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially on a challenging loop like this, as services might be sparse between villages.
